video.title: Stop Asking How Many Days You Should Train (Do This Instead)
video.description:
Most people are asking the wrong question about training frequency.

"How many days should I train?" sounds like the right question — but it's actually holding you back. In this video I break down the only training framework that matters: consistency over frequency, and progressive overload as the real driver of results.

What you'll learn:
• Why the "how many days?" question is setting you up to fail
• The two things that actually determine if you make progress
• How to build a training schedule around your real life
• What most people miss about progressive overload
• Why showing up imperfectly beats the perfect plan you never start

This is not about training 6 days a week. It's about building a system that doesn't fall apart.

Timestamps:
00:00 The wrong question
01:15 Why frequency is a distraction
02:40 The two things that matter
04:10 Consistency: what it actually means
05:50 Progressive overload: the real driver
07:30 How to build your schedule
08:45 The principle that ties it together
09:30 Recap
